page: Amazon Web Services r8a.large is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, configured with 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU metrics, Geekbench single-core scores, and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression rates all placing in the top 10% tier. Conversely, multi-core performance metrics, such as stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores, fall within the average range. Memory bandwidth tests for both small cached and large uncached blocks also demonstrate average tier performance. Weaknesses are observed in software compilation with Clang and ray tracing workloads, which rank in the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8a.large offers a dense DDR5 memory allocation per vCPU, making it suitable for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel processing. However, the lack of local NVMe storage and the modest 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth represent notable tradeoffs for data-intensive operations. This instance is suited for workloads such as medium-scale database operations, text processing, caching layers, and single-threaded data manipulation tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r8a.large is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 16.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark results highlight top-tier single-core CPU and text processing performance, while multi-core execution and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. Tradeoffs include weaker performance in software compilation and ray tracing. This hardware profile is suited for memory-sensitive, single-threaded workloads, text processing, and database operations that do not require local NVMe storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8a.large is a memory-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR5 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 9R45 CPU, delivering top-tier single-core performance for memory-bound workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8a.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR5 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual instance featuring high-speed DDR5 RAM and strong single-core performance for memory-bound and text-processing workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 9R45 processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs at 4.5 GHz
16.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory (8.0 GB per core)
Top-tier single-core CPU and text processing benchmark results
No local storage and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Suited for database operations, caching, and single-threaded tasks
